Despite recording 2,563 total yards and 26 touchdowns, his completion percentage dipped to 56.5%, highlighting ongoing struggles with pocket processing and intermediate accuracy. Scouts remain enamored with his "tank-like" running style and elite arm strength, but his high turnover-worthy play rate and a challenging East-West Shrine Bowl week led to a surprising 2026 NFL Combine snub. Now viewed as a priority free agent or 7th-round flyer, Drones is a classic high-upside project. He possesses the raw physical tools of an NFL starter but requires significant mechanical refinement and a patient coaching staff to unlock his full potential at the next level.

name: David Blay Jr.
summary: David Blay Jr. is a battle-tested interior defensive lineman who capped a nomadic five-year collegiate journey by helping lead the Miami Hurricanes to the 2026 National Championship game. Measuring 6-foot-4 and 305 pounds, Blay's primary strengths are his disciplined gap integrity and elite run-stuffing ability, showcased by his capacity to control the point of attack against high-level ACC competition. While his 2024 season at Louisiana Tech proved his ceiling as a pass rusher (6.5 sacks), his role at Miami shifted toward a rotational anchor. His draft potential is currently viewed as a Day 3/Priority Free Agent selection for the 2026 NFL Draft. Scouts cite a lack of consistent, explosive twitch and a late-season undisclosed injury as primary weaknesses.

name: Kansei Matsuzawa
summary: Kansei Matsuzawa (5’11/200) became a household name in 2025, shattering the FBS record with 25 consecutive field goal makes to start a season for Hawaii. This Lou Groza Award finalist finished the regular season with a staggering 96.2% accuracy rate, proving his "ice-water" composure in high-leverage scenarios. While his "long" of 52 yards is respectable, his elite consistency from 45 yards and in makes him a primary draft target for specialist-needy teams. Following a "perfect" week during the recent showcase circuit, Matsuzawa is trending toward a rare fifth-round selection. He is a "franchise-leg" prospect with the mental toughness and technical precision needed to erase field-position disadvantages and win games from the logo at the next level.

name: Jack Strand
summary: Jack Strand (6’5/240) emerged as the "FCS-to-NFL" sleeper of the 2026 cycle after shattering conference records with 12,134 passing yards and 115 touchdowns at MSUM. This prototypical pocket commander combines elite size with the "touch" required to make mid-range NFL throws, recording a staggering 42 touchdowns in his final season. While scouts note his lower-body mechanics remain "unpolished" and his footwork can be "bouncy" under pressure, his 63% completion rate and ability to escape pressure suggest a high-upside developmental floor. Currently trending as a Day 3 gem, Strand possesses the raw arm talent and "Tyson Bagent-style" grit needed to revolutionize a creative NFL offensive system. He is the premier small-school playmaker with a massive professional ceiling.

name: Rene Konga
summary: Raneiria "Rene" Kings (5’9/175) emerged as the premier "gadget" weapon in the Group of Five, recording a dynamic 2025 campaign for North Texas with over 1,100 all-purpose yards. Combining elite 4.39-speed with the vision to erase pursuit angles in space, Kings functions as a primary separator who thrives on jet sweeps and screen patterns. While his diminutive frame restricts him to a "rotational" NFL slot role, his high-volume production and return-game utility highlight a "game-breaking" floor. Currently trending as a Day 3 sleeper, Kings offers the "Tyreek Hill-esque" acceleration and short-area quickness needed to transform an offense’s explosive-play rate. He is a premier one-cut playmaker with the rare twitch to turn routine touches into house-calls.

name: Daniel Harris
summary: Daniel Harris (6’3/195) stands out as a "high-ceiling" secondary project who showcased flashes of dominance during his junior campaign at Georgia. This Miami native utilizes elite length and a 10.74-second 100-meter speed profile to pattern-match vertical threats in the SEC. While he has primarily operated in a rotational role—recording 22 stops and three breakups—his physical "alpha" traits and track-star recovery burst have NFL scouts projecting a major Day 3 riser. Currently vaulting into late-round consideration, Harris offers the rare frame and "click-and-close" agility required to eventually neutralize physical NFL "X" receivers. He is a high-upside developmental prospect with the raw physical tools to dominate in a professional press-man system.

name: RJ Maryland
summary: RJ Maryland is a 6'4", 240-pound tight end from SMU who enters the 2026 draft cycle as the Mustangs' all-time leader in receiving yards and touchdowns for his position. A fluid, explosive athlete with a background as a former wide receiver, Maryland is prized for his "matchup-nightmare" potential, combining nuanced route-running with an elite catch radius and strong hands. While his 2025 production (322 yards, 2 TDs) was quieter than his breakout sophomore campaign, scouts view him as a premier "big-play" threat who excels in contested-catch situations and creates significant yards after the catch. He has officially been invited to the 2026 NFL Scouting Combine, where his athletic testing is expected to solidify him as a Day 2 (Round 2–3) prospect. As a high-IQ player with NFL lineage, Maryland is a favorite for teams seeking a versatile, move-tight end who can win in the slot or in-line.

name: Karson Sharar
summary: Karson Sharar is a disciplined 6'2", 235-pound linebacker for the Iowa Hawkeyes who enjoyed a significant breakout during the 2025 season. After spending several years as a rotational player and special teams contributor, Sharar started all 13 games as a senior, recording 83 total tackles, 12 tackles for loss, and 4 sacks. Scouts highlight his "violent" downhill play style and positional versatility, particularly his effectiveness as a "spy" linebacker—headlined by a two-sack performance against Vanderbilt in the ReliaQuest Bowl. Known for his high football IQ and relentless motor, Sharar recently earned an invitation to the 2026 NFL Scouting Combine. His ability to navigate blocks and excel in the "Will" linebacker role makes him a rising prospect for the 2026 NFL Draft.

name: Fred Davis II
summary: Fred Davis II enters the 2026 NFL Draft cycle as a seasoned, "twitchy" boundary cornerback with a massive 6'1", 200-pound frame. After a winding collegiate journey through Clemson, UCF, and Jacksonville State, Davis solidified his stock at Northwestern, where he utilized his elite length and 4.4-level speed to thrive in a pro-style system. His primary strengths include exceptional press-man technique, fluid transitions for his size, and aggressive ball skills that allow him to disrupt passing lanes effectively. However, scouts note weaknesses in his off-ball awareness—occasionally losing track of receivers when "eye-locked" on the quarterback— and a history of lower-body injuries that limited his early-career production. Currently projected as a mid-round developmental prospect with high-end starter upside, his invitation to the 2026 Senior Bowl suggests he is a riser.

name: Jack Velling
summary: Jack Velling (6’5/244) contributed as a rotational tight end for Michigan State in 2025, showing receptions in seam and red-zone roles with reliable hands and blocking effort on short-yardage plays. Route nuance and leaping at the catch point appeared in limited production. Developmental in-line/move tight end with receiving traits and every-down value. Blocking technique and anchor remain inconsistent against power edges; height advantage is limited in contested situations; vertical speed to stretch seams is modest. Day 3 projection (Round 4-6) in the 2026 NFL Draft with upside as rotational TE2/TE3 if blocking hand placement is refined and strength is added.

name: Dean Connors
summary: Dean Connors is a 6'0", 208-pound "all-purpose" back whose stock soared after a dominant 2026 East-West Shrine Bowl performance. A transfer from Rice to Houston, Connors capped his 2025 senior season with 977 rushing yards and 9 total touchdowns, proving his durability as a lead back. Scouts laud his "sure-handed" reliability—remaining turnover-free throughout his career—and his sophisticated route-running, which saw him haul in 139 passes over the last three seasons. While he lacks a true "getaway" gear for long-distance scores, his patience behind blockers and physical "churn" through contact have him trending as a viable RB2 at the next level. Previously overlooked, Connors is now a Day 3 riser likely to be selected in the 5th or 6th round by a team seeking a pro-ready, three-down contributor.

name: Caden Fordham
summary: Caden Fordham (6’4”, 235 lbs) profiles as a modern receiving tight end, producing 30+ receptions, 400+ yards, and multiple touchdowns while working seams and underneath zones. He moves fluidly for his size, flashes natural hands, and understands spacing against coverage. Fordham’s blocking technique and strength remain developmental, particularly versus NFL edge defenders, but effort is consistent. He stayed under the radar without Senior Bowl exposure. He projects as a Round 6 pick, contributing early as a TE3 and situational receiver, with upside if his functional strength improves.

name: Squirrel White
summary: Squirrel White (5’10/175 est.) contributed in Tennessee’s slot in 2025, displaying quick separation underneath, reliable hands, and yards after catch on slants/screens. Route nuance, acceleration out of breaks, and space creation appeared in limited production. Slot traits and return potential provide developmental upside. Size and contested-catch ability limit him against physical corners; deep-threat profile remains modest; press coverage at the line creates inconsistency. Day 3 projection (Round 5-7) in the 2026 NFL Draft with upside as rotational slot piece or special teams contributor if releases are refined and strength is added.

name: Kolbey Taylor
summary: Kolbey Taylor is a long, physically imposing cornerback standing 6'3" and weighing 190 lbs, making him a intriguing mid-to-late round prospect for the 2026 NFL Draft. After transferring from Wyoming to Vanderbilt, Taylor has utilized his elite frame and "twitchy" athleticism to compete against SEC-level competition, earning a spot on the 2024 and 2025 "Freaks List" for his raw physical tools. His strengths include aggressive press-man coverage, a high motor as a tackler, and a massive wingspan that disrupts passing lanes. However, his weaknesses lie in inconsistent spatial awareness when his back is turned to the quarterback and occasional lapses in technique against smaller, quicker receivers. While he has shown flashes of being a future NFL contributor, he remains a developmental "size-speed" project for teams seeking a boundary specialist.

name: Cam Miller
summary: Cam Miller (6’0/192) stands out as a "high-IQ" defensive general who utilized a stellar 2025 season at Rutgers to cement his status as a premiere secondary anchor. Recording 47 tackles, 6 pass breakups, and a sack, Miller combines elite "click-and-close" agility with the versatility to excel in both nickel and deep-third roles. While he lacks the "elite" physical mass of a traditional box safety, his diagnostic instincts and "alpha" communication skills were the talk of the 2026 Shrine Bowl. Currently projected as a mid-round value, Miller offers the high-floor reliability and special teams utility required to impact an NFL roster immediately. He is a pro-ready "football junkie" with the ball-skills to become a consistent turnover generator.

name: Kyle Walljasper
summary: Kyle Walljasper (6’1/230) enters the 2026 cycle as the most decorated small-school prospect in the nation, having shattered D-II records at Minnesota-Duluth with over 1,000 yards rushing and passing in consecutive seasons. This "power-built" signal-caller operates with a punishing run style reminiscent of a modern fullback, utilizing elite contact balance to overwhelm smaller defenders. While evaluators note the significant "jump in competition" remains his primary hurdle, his 38 total touchdowns in 2025 and "piston-like" leg drive have earned him a Senior Bowl roster spot. Projected as a priority UDFA or Day 3 sleeper, Walljasper possesses the rare physical toughness and "blue-collar" motor required to potentially successfully transition into a hybrid H-back or developmental NFL quarterback role.

name: Kahlil Saunders
summary: Kahlil Saunders is a massive, reliable interior presence who anchored the Kentucky defensive line through the 2025 season. Standing 6-foot-5 and 293 pounds, his primary strengths are his elite functional strength and block-shedding ability, which make him a consistent force against the run in 3-4 schemes. He demonstrated significant durability and leadership as a multi-game captain, notably peaking with a career-high six tackles against Georgia. His draft potential is currently projected as a Day 3 selection (Ranked ~333 overall), appealing to teams looking for a high-motor rotational tackle. Weaknesses include limited high-end sack production and a need for more diverse pass-rush moves to complement his natural power at the professional level.

name: Malik Spencer
summary: Malik Spencer is a versatile, high-motor safety prospect for the 2026 NFL Draft, currently projected as a top-50 talent with mid-to-early round potential. Measuring 6'1" and 193 lbs, Spencer has established himself as a resilient leader in the Spartans' secondary, bouncing back from a season-ending 2024 injury to start every game in 2025. His strengths include elite toughness, a "plug-and-play" versatility across the defensive backfield, and a relentless motor evidenced by his 121 career tackles and improved pass disruption. However, scouts view his weaknesses as occasional over-aggression that led to a 2024 targeting ejection and a need for more consistent elite ball production to solidify a first-round grade. With 4.50 speed and special teams grit, he is a rising senior to watch.

name: Mel Brown
summary: Mel Brown is a 5'9", 180-lb running back and return specialist who recently transferred to Colorado State for his final season. Known for his explosive, "home-run" speed, Brown averaged an impressive 5.7 yards per carry at UConn in 2024 and was a standout kick returner, highlighted by a 96-yard touchdown return. Despite a collarbone injury that limited him to just four games in 2025, he remained highly efficient, averaging over 8 yards per touch when healthy. Scouts value his low center of gravity, vision, and versatile playmaking ability out of the backfield. Having earned a spot on the 2026 Senior Bowl Top 300, Brown is considered a dynamic "spark plug" prospect for the NFL.

name: Al'Zillion Hamilton
summary: Al’Zillion Hamilton is a veteran 5'11", 185-pound cornerback for the Fresno State Bulldogs who served as a primary defensive anchor during the 2025 season. A senior leader with over 40 games of experience, Hamilton recorded 38 total tackles and 4 interceptions in 2025, further establishing his reputation as a "lockdown" defender in the Mountain West. Known for his explosive speed and high football IQ, he was named to the Jim Thorpe Award Watch List and consistently ranked among the FBS leaders in passes defended. Scouts value his "speed-to-ball" instincts and vocal leadership on the field. Having refined his skills under elite competition, Hamilton projects as a reliable, technically sound prospect for the 2026 NFL Draft.

name: Wydett Williams Jr.
summary: Wydett Williams (6’2/210) functions as a "range-heavy" enforcer who utilized a 2025 transfer to Ole Miss to cement his status as a premiere deep-third defender. This former ULM standout operates with a "seek-and-destroy" mentality, combining 99 career tackles with the ball-skills needed to record three interceptions in a single season. While he needs to improve his tackling consistency against heavy-set blockers, his 4.52-speed and natural diagnostic instincts against vertical SEC threats impressed evaluators during the recent showcase circuit. Currently projected as a mid-round value, Williams offers the high-floor "centerfield" safety traits and special teams floor needed to stabilize an NFL secondary. He is a pro-ready defensive general with the elite communication skills required for a starting role.

name: Nikai Martinez
summary: Nikai Martinez is a highly experienced, high-IQ safety prospect for the 2026 NFL Draft, currently projected as a mid-round (Day 3) developmental talent with special teams upside. Measuring 5'11" and 195 lbs, Martinez has logged nearly 2,000 career defensive snaps across UCF and Michigan State. His strengths include exceptional reliability in open-field tackling, high-level versatility to play both nickel and deep safety, and a "nose for the ball" that resulted in five career interceptions. However, his weaknesses involve a smaller frame that can be overwhelmed by power-back blockers and a history of lower-body injuries that limited his 2025 campaign to just four games. If he demonstrates full health at the NFL Combine, his veteran leadership and ball skills make him a strong rotational candidate.

name: Micah Davey
summary: Micah Davey is a prolific 6'2", 235-pound linebacker who recently transitioned to the UTEP Miners after a historic career at McNeese State. A premier tackling machine, Davey led the FCS in multiple categories during his 2023 All-American campaign, recording a staggering 153 total tackles. In the 2025 season, he maintained his elite production, surpassing 300 career tackles and displaying a unique "nose for the football" that allows him to consistently diagnose plays. Scouts admire his sideline-to-sideline range and high football IQ—pedigree he likely shares with his father, former LSU quarterback Rohan Davey. With his blend of collegiate experience and proven disruptive ability, Davey enters the 2026 NFL Draft cycle as one of the most productive linebacker prospects in the nation.

name: Jeremiah Franklin
summary: Jeremiah Franklin is a reliable and balanced tight end who significantly elevated his draft stock during the 2025 season. Standing 6'3" and weighing 248 pounds, the former three-star recruit developed from a situational player into an All-ACC Third Team performer. He recorded career highs in 2025 with 49 receptions for 506 yards, proving to be a consistent "move" tight end with reliable hands and a high catch rate. Scouts value his versatility as a blocker, where he utilizes his sturdy frame to seal edges in the run game. While not the most elite vertical threat in the 2026 class, his consistent development and ability to function as a safety valve over the middle earned him a 2026 NFL Scouting Combine invitation.

name: Jacob De Jesus
summary: Jacob De Jesus is a 5'7", 175-lb wide receiver and return specialist who recently completed a record-breaking senior season at Cal after transferring from UNLV. A premier slot technician, De Jesus led the ACC in 2025 with 108 receptions for 1,030 yards, showcasing elite short-area quickness and reliable hands. Beyond his receiving production, he is one of the nation’s most decorated special teams weapons, earning First-Team All-ACC honors as both an all-purpose player and specialist. Scouts value his "spark plug" playmaking ability and high football IQ, though his small stature remains a primary concern for NFL transition. Despite size limitations, his consistent production and 1,645 all-purpose yards make him a compelling 2026 NFL Draft sleeper.

name: Karon Prunty
summary: Karon Prunty is a lengthy, technically sound cornerback standing 6'2" and weighing 192 lbs, currently projected as a mid-round prospect for the 2026 NFL Draft. After a prolific journey through Kansas and North Carolina A&T, Prunty excelled as a Redshirt Senior at Wake Forest, earning Third Team All-ACC honors. His strengths include elite catch-rate prevention (44.4% in 2025), high-level press-man ability, and veteran play-recognition that allows him to disrupt passing lanes. However, his weaknesses center on a high-volume transfer history and past medical absences that raise questions about long-term durability. While his size and statistical dominance in coverage are NFL-ready, scouts will closely monitor his athletic testing to confirm if his speed matches his elite frame.

name: Stephen Hall
summary: Stephen Hall is a physically imposing boundary cornerback standing 6'0" and weighing 202 lbs, projected as a late-round developmental prospect for the 2026 NFL Draft. A graduate transfer from Washington State and a former team captain, Hall brought veteran stability to the Missouri secondary in 2025. His primary strengths lie in his physicality at the line of scrimmage, reliable open-field tackling (evidenced by 70+ career tackles), and high-level special teams value. However, scouts highlight weaknesses in his long speed and a history of lower-body injuries during his early collegiate career that hampered his consistency. While he lacks elite recovery "burst," his frame and leadership qualities make him an intriguing candidate for teams utilizing a heavy zone or press-coverage scheme.

name: Emmanuel Henderson
summary: Emmanuel Henderson (5’11”, 200 lbs) brings explosive ability to the backfield, totaling 700+ rushing yards, receiving production, and return value. He shows excellent acceleration, creativity in space, and the ability to flip field position. Henderson’s size limits durability concerns and pass-protection reliability, but his playmaking stands out. He projects as a Round 6–7 pick, contributing early as a change-of-pace back, return specialist, and offensive weapon, particularly in spread or tempo-based systems.

name: Tyre West
summary: Tyre West is a powerful and versatile defensive lineman who has declared for the 2026 NFL Draft following a consistent four-year career at Tennessee. Measuring 6-foot-3 and 290 pounds, West’s primary strengths include his explosive linear power and ability to disrupt backfields, evidenced by his career-high 7.5 tackles for loss and 4 sacks in 2025. He is a high-motor defender who thrives in heavy rotations, showing the strength to set the edge and the versatility to slide inside. His draft potential is currently projected as a Day 3 selection, likely appealing to teams seeking a scheme-versatile rotational piece. Weaknesses include a need for greater technical refinement in his pass-rush plan and more consistent production against elite SEC double-teams.

name: Grayson James
summary: Grayson James is a 6'3", 226-lb quarterback known for his physical toughness and "team-first" mentality. A seasoned senior with starting experience at both FIU and Boston College, James is characterized by his solid arm strength and ability to maintain poise under pressure. While he has demonstrated a "clutch gene" in relief roles, leading comebacks with his dual-threat mobility, he has faced challenges with consistency and turnovers, posting a 57.6% completion rate and a nearly 1:1 touchdown-to-interception ratio in 2025. Scouts view him as a battle-tested gamer who excels in leadership and extra preparation, though his tendency to force throws indicates a need for better decision-making to succeed at the next level.

name: Logan Diggs
summary: Logan Diggs is a 6'0", 215-lb running back for Ole Miss who has proven his resilience after recovering from a serious knee injury sustained in early 2024. A battle-tested veteran with experience at Notre Dame and LSU, Diggs is a quintessential downhill, one-cut runner who excels between the tackles. He possesses a powerful base and excellent contact balance, allowing him to finish runs with violence at the second level. Beyond his rushing utility, scouts highlight his elite pass protection and reliability as a receiver out of the backfield. While he lacks top-end breakaway speed, his high-floor "bruiser" profile and leadership make him a projected Day 3 pick for the 2026 NFL Draft.

name: Dalton Johnson
summary: Dalton Johnson (6’3”, 235 lbs) plays with physicality and discipline at linebacker, finishing with 90+ tackles, tackles for loss, and pressure production as a blitzer. He reads keys quickly, fills downhill with authority, and brings leadership to the front seven. Johnson’s lateral range is adequate rather than dynamic, and coverage ability is role-dependent. He earned All-Conference recognition for consistent production. He projects as a Round 5–6 pick, contributing early on special teams and early-down defense, with rotational starter potential in base packages.

name: Tre Smith
summary: Tre Smith is a powerful 6'5", 259-pound edge rusher for the Arizona Wildcats who has proven to be a cornerstone of their defensive front. A former standout at San Jose State, Smith made an immediate impact in the Big 12, starting all 12 games in 2024 and leading the Wildcats with 53 tackles, 7.5 tackles for loss, and 4.5 sacks. Scouts are impressed by his "speed-to-power" transition and his consistent ability to force turnovers, highlighted by his disruptive performance against Northern Arizona. Although his 2025 season was cut short by injury in October, his high motor and veteran experience—evidenced by 10 quarterback hurries in limited early-season action—keep him firmly on the radar for the 2026 NFL Draft.

name: Kalil Alexander
summary: Kalil Alexander (6’3/310) enters the 2026 cycle as one of the most "explosive" interior sleepers in the country after a dominant 2025 breakout at Texas State. Utilizing a devastating "first-step" quickness and "heavy hands," Alexander consistently collapses pocket integrity from the 3-technique alignment, recording 6.5 sacks this past season. While evaluators note he must continue adding functional mass to withstand the "grind" of NFL double-teams, his ability to "forklift" centers off their spots highlights a legitimate pro-ready floor. Currently projected as a Day 3 value, Alexander offers the rare lateral twitch and "whistle-to-whistle" motor required to revolutionize an interior NFL rotation. He is a high-upside disruptor with the technical savvy to out-leverage veteran professional offensive linemen.

name: Walker Eget
summary: Walker Eget is a 6'3", 223-lb quarterback who recently committed to Duke for the 2026 season following a breakout year at San José State. A prototypical pocket passer with prototypical size, Eget is lauded for his elite arm talent, finishing 2025 with 3,047 passing yards and 17 touchdowns. He excels in high-volume passing attacks, evidenced by massive single-game performances of 473 and 458 yards. While he demonstrates a high "big-time throw" rate and keeps a low pressure-to-sack ratio, scouts note that his aggressive style can lead to "turnover-worthy plays." Despite an injured leg that sidelined him late in 2025, his experience and 30 career touchdowns make him a high-floor starter capable of stabilizing a Power 4 offense.

name: Donavon Greene
summary: Donavon Greene is an explosive vertical threat with a lean, 6'2" frame and long arms that facilitate high-level contested catches. A former four-star recruit and basketball standout, he possesses elite leaping ability and short-area burst, evidenced by his career average of over 18.0 yards per reception. While he is a dominant deep-ball tracker who can separate downfield, his technical route running and hands remain areas for refinement. His career has been defined by significant production—including a record-breaking 20.1 YPC season at Wake Forest—tempered by frequent injuries, including a torn ACL. After transferring to Virginia Tech for the 2025 season, Greene displayed his typical big-play potential before stepping away from the program in November.

name: Alex Afari Jr.
summary: Alex Afari Jr (6’2/227) solidified his status as a "range-heavy" enforcer during his senior season at Kentucky, recording 61 tackles and a "dominant" 13-tackle performance against Toledo. This versatile linebacker/safety hybrid operates with a "seek-and-destroy" mentality, utilizing 7.0 TFLs and 2.5 sacks to disrupt offensive timing from multiple alignments. While scouts question his "elite" vertical range in deep-third zone, his 11.0 TFLs in 2024 and improved "post-pressure" poise have earned him a mid-round grade for 2026. Currently trending as a Day 3 riser, Afari offers the physical play-strength and special teams floor required to impact an NFL roster immediately. He is a high-IQ defensive general with the "thumping" power to stabilize a professional sub-package rotation.

name: Darian Chestnut
summary: Darian "Duce" Chestnut is a versatile, instinctual defensive back standing 6'0" and 200 lbs, currently projected as a mid-round (Day 3) prospect for the 2026 NFL Draft. After a productive career at Syracuse and a brief stint at LSU, Chestnut returned to the Orange, where he transitioned from a Freshman All-American cornerback to a veteran safety. His primary strengths include elite ball-tracking skills—evidenced by six career interceptions—aggressive run support, and the "twitchy" athleticism to play both press-man and deep zone. However, his weaknesses center on inconsistent top-end speed against vertical threats and a history of lower-body injuries that limited his 2023 campaign. Despite these concerns, his high football IQ and special teams experience make him a high-floor developmental secondary piece.

name: Damonic Williams
summary: Damonic Williams is a robust interior defensive lineman who finished his collegiate career as a vocal leader and team captain for the Oklahoma Sooners. Standing 6-foot-1 and 323 pounds, his primary strengths are his immense functional power and leverage, which allow him to anchor effectively against double teams and maintain gap integrity in the SEC. A former Freshman All-American at TCU, he brings a wealth of starting experience and a high football IQ to the trenches. His draft potential is currently projected as a Day 3 selection (Ranked ~294 overall). Weaknesses include limited length for the position and a lack of high-end lateral twitch, which can cap his effectiveness as a consistent pass-rush threat at the NFL level.

name: Dashawn Jones
summary: DaShawn Jones is an experienced, instinctual defensive back standing 6'0" and weighing 190 lbs, currently projected as a mid-to-late round prospect for the 2026 NFL Draft. After a productive three-year stint at Wake Forest, Jones transferred to Alabama, where he showcased his "veteran savvy" and versatility by playing both outside corner and the "Husky" (nickel) role. His strengths include elite ball-tracking skills—highlighted by an 18-yard pick-six against South Carolina in 2025—and a steady, professional approach that made him a leader in the Tide’s secondary. However, his weaknesses involve being less naturally "freakish" than younger blue-chip prospects and a history of lower-body injuries that landed him on the availability report late in his final season.

name: Al-Jay Henderson
summary: Al-Jay Henderson is a 5'10", 216-lb running back who recently concluded a productive senior campaign at Buffalo. A physical workhorse with a low center of gravity, Henderson is a "North-South" runner who excels at gaining yards after contact, evidenced by his 1,078-yard breakout season in 2024. While his efficiency dipped slightly in 2025 to 4.1 yards per carry, he remained a primary offensive focal point, recording 755 rushing yards and six touchdowns. Scouts appreciate his reliable hands and "receiving upside," as he tallied 44 receptions over his final two seasons. Although he lacks elite breakaway speed, his durability, Maxwell Award watch list pedigree, and ability to contribute on all three downs make him a dependable rotational prospect for the 2026 NFL Draft.

name: Chris Hilton Jr.
summary: Chris Hilton Jr. is an elite vertical threat whose game is built on world-class speed and "gravity-defying" leaping ability. A former state high jump champion, the 6'0", 190-pound receiver thrives as a deep-ball specialist, evidenced by a career average of over 20 yards per reception and multiple scores exceeding 40 yards. While a history of lower-leg injuries limited his collegiate availability, he demonstrated high-end durability during the 2026 draft cycle by becoming the first player to compete in four pre-draft all-star games (including the Senior Bowl). Scouts value his precise route running and 4.31-range speed, though he must continue to add functional strength to transition his "big-play" identity to the professional level.

name: Gunner Maldonado
summary: Gunner Maldonado is a gritty, high-IQ safety prospect for the 2026 NFL Draft, currently projected as a Day 3 developmental talent. Standing 5'11" and 193 lbs, Maldonado concluded a productive six-year collegiate career with stops at Northwestern and Arizona before finishing at Kansas State. His strengths include elite "box safety" instincts, a relentless motor in run support, and proven ball-hawking ability—highlighted by his 2023 Alamo Bowl Defensive MVP honors and multiple career forced fumbles. However, his weaknesses involve a limited physical ceiling and a history of lower-body injuries, including a season-ending leg injury in 2024. While he lacks top-tier recovery speed, his veteran leadership and reliability as a downhill tackler make him a viable special teams contributor at the next level.

name: Tanner Arkin
summary: Tanner Arkin (6’5/245 est.) contributed as a rotational tight end for Illinois in 2025, showing receptions in seam and red-zone roles with reliable hands and blocking effort on short-yardage plays. Route nuance and leaping at the catch point appeared in limited production. Developmental in-line/move tight end with receiving traits and every-down value. Blocking technique and anchor remain inconsistent against power edges; height advantage is limited in contested situations; vertical speed to stretch seams is modest. Day 3 projection (Round 4-6) in the 2026 NFL Draft with upside as rotational TE2/TE3 if blocking hand placement is refined and strength is added.

name: Mo Westmoreland
summary: Maurice "Mo" Westmoreland is a high-motor edge rusher who established himself as a dominant force in Conference USA before transferring to Tulane for the 2025 season. Standing 6'2" and 250 lbs, Westmoreland is characterized by a relentless motor and natural pass-rushing instincts, evidenced by his 15.5 career sacks at UTEP. His primary strengths include his versatility to play as a stand-up outside linebacker and his proven production as a back-to-back First-Team All-CUSA selection. However, scouts cite his lack of elite size as a weakness, which can make it difficult for him to anchor in a traditional three-point stance against larger tackles. Projected as a Day 3 selection (Round 5-6), his stock depends on his 2026 pre-draft testing.

name: Amari Jackson
summary: Amari Jackson is an aggressive, press-man specialist who recently transferred to Maryland after a productive career at Boston College. His primary strength lies in his elite competitiveness and physical nature; he thrives in the contact window, using a powerful upper body to disrupt receivers at the line of scrimmage. Jackson demonstrates excellent downhill triggers and instincts in zone coverage, making him a reliable tackler and a constant threat to tunnel screens. However, his draft potential—currently projected as a Day 3/Scheme Specific prospect—is tempered by below-average length and a history of playing on his heels. Scouts also note concerns regarding his recovery from a 2024 ACL injury, though his high football IQ and leadership remain significant assets for a 2026 NFL Draft profile.

name: Maverick McIvor
summary: Maverick McIvor is a 6'2", 210-lb quarterback who recently capped off a prolific collegiate career at Western Kentucky. A quintessential "gunslinger," McIvor is a high-volume pocket passer who thrived in Air Raid systems, most notably during a standout 2024 season at Abilene Christian where he threw for 3,828 yards and 30 touchdowns. In 2025, he led the Hilltoppers to a New Orleans Bowl victory, earning Bowl MVP honors despite a mid-season shoulder injury that limited him to eight games. Scouts admire his arm talent, touch, and ability to manage the blitz, though his older age (25) and limited mobility are potential draft hurdles. He finished his final season with a 67% completion rate, proving to be a reliable, battle-tested leader for the next level.

name: Devin Mockobee
summary: Devin Mockobee is a 6'0", 202-lb senior running back at Purdue who transitioned from a walk-on to one of the most productive rushers in program history. A high-IQ "one-cut" runner, Mockobee is lauded for his elite vision and ability to navigate through traffic in both zone and gap schemes. Before a season-ending ankle injury in late 2025, he tallied 521 rushing yards and four touchdowns, averaging 4.2 yards per carry while contributing significantly as a receiver with 215 yards. Scouts value his reliable ball security and versatility as a third-down outlet, though he lacks elite top-end breakaway speed. Projected as a Day 3 selection for the 2026 NFL Draft, he offers high-floor potential as a dependable rotational back.

name: Devonte Ross
summary: Devonte Ross is a dynamic, undersized playmaker known for his game-breaking speed and versatility. After a breakout 2024 season at Troy where he set a school record with 229 receiving yards in a single game and earned first-team All-Sun Belt honors, he transferred to Penn State for his final eligibility year. Measuring 5'11" and roughly 170 pounds, Ross is a vertical threat who excels at getting behind defenses, though his slight frame can struggle with the physicality of Power Conference defensive backs. He is equally dangerous as a return specialist, having recorded a 77-yard punt return touchdown. While his route running and catch radius are areas for growth, his elite acceleration makes him a constant threat for explosive plays.

name: Jimmori Robinson
summary: Jimmori Robinson is a physically gifted edge rusher who transferred to West Virginia after a prolific tenure at UTSA. Measuring 6'4" and 262 lbs, Robinson’s primary strength is his explosive backfield disruption, highlighted by a 2024 season where he recorded 10.5 sacks and a school-record 5.5 tackles for loss in a single game. His length and "virtuoso" pass-rushing instincts make him a constant threat to opposing quarterbacks. However, his limited starting experience (13 starts in 43 games) and the need to adjust his conditioning to a higher level of competition are noted weaknesses. Currently projected as a late-round flyer (Round 6-7) or priority free agent, his draft value depends on his 2026 pre-draft measurements and consistency.

name: Rohan Jones
summary: Rohan Jones is a prototypical modern-day tight end who combines elite athleticism with a physical edge. Standing 6'3" and weighing 242 pounds, the Montreal native took a unique path to the SEC, with standout stops at Maine and Montana State. He is a dangerous vertical threat, finishing the 2025 season with a remarkable 27.3 yards per reception and an exceptional PFF receiving grade. Jones excels at creating mismatches using wide receiver-like speed—reportedly in the mid-4.4 range—and high-pointing the ball with a 66.7% contested catch rate. While he is highly effective as a "move" tight end, scouts also praise his willingness to engage as a lead blocker in the run game.

name: Trinidad Chambliss
summary: Trinidad Chambliss (6’1/200) stands as the most debated signal-caller in the 2026 cycle following a historic playoff run that nearly saw him return to Oxford for another season. After leading Ole Miss to the CFP Semifinals with 3,937 passing yards and 30 total touchdowns, Chambliss initially sought a sixth year of eligibility. However, following the NCAA's denial on January 9, 2026—and despite an ongoing legal battle for an injunction—he is currently projected as a top-three quarterback for the 2026 NFL Draft. Scouts rave about his "Russell Wilson-style" dual-threat utility and elite 66.1% completion rate against SEC defenses. While smaller in frame, his "ice-water" poise in the Fiesta Bowl has scouts viewing him as a legitimate first-round talent.

name: Curtis Allen
summary: Curtis Allen (6’2/215) is a physically imposing running back who dominated the 2025 season, earning the Harlon Hill Trophy and Walter Camp DII Player of the Year honors. Known as "The Quiet Storm," Allen combines elite vision, patience, and power to turn routine plays into explosive gains, evidenced by his record-breaking 2,409 rushing yards and 30 touchdowns. Scouts view him as a "D2 Derrick Henry" due to his ability to break tackles and maintain breakaway speed, highlighted by multiple 80-plus yard runs. While he transitioned from a supporting role to a national standout, his consistency—rushing for over 100 yards in every game—demonstrates a high floor. His rare blend of size and production makes him a premier small-school prospect.

name: Drew Stevens
summary: Drew Stevens (6’0/190 est.) started as Iowa’s kicker in 2025, showing reliable accuracy on field goals, touch on kickoffs, and consistency in big moments. Developmental specialist with starter traits if leg strength and range are refined. Weaknesses include occasional misses from distance or in adverse weather; kickoff placement and hang time need improvement; limited experience in high-stakes scenarios. Day 3/UDFA projection in the 2026 NFL Draft with upside as depth kicker or special teams contributor if consistency and leg power improve.

name: Devin Voisin
summary: Devin Voisin is a 5'10", 187-pound "technician" from South Alabama whose draft stock is steadily gaining steam after a productive 2026 Senior Bowl week in his hometown of Mobile. A seven-year collegiate veteran who overcame a major knee injury in 2023, Voisin returned to peak form in 2025, hauling in 67 receptions for 775 yards and showcasing his reliability as a primary punt returner. Scouts are enamored with his "pro-ready" route running and elite hands, evidenced by several high-difficulty catches during Senior Bowl practices that proved he could separate against Power Four defensive backs. While he lacks elite size and "burner" speed, his high football IQ and special teams versatility make him a high-floor prospect. Currently projected as a Day 3 pick or high-priority free agent, Voisin is a favorite for teams seeking a dependable slot target and a core special teams contributor.

name: Will Ferrin
summary: Will Ferrin (6’0/190 est.) served as BYU’s starting kicker in 2025, demonstrating strong leg strength, accuracy on field goals and kickoffs, and consistency in pressure situations. Developmental specialist with starter traits if range and touch are refined. Weaknesses include occasional misses from distance or in wind; kickoff hang time and placement need polish; limited experience in high-pressure playoff environments. Day 3/UDFA projection in the 2026 NFL Draft with upside as depth kicker or special teams contributor if leg strength and consistency translate.

name: Max Bredeson
summary: Max Bredeson (6'2", 250 lbs) is a throwback "utility" prospect from Michigan who has redefined the fullback position for the modern era. The 2025 Lowman Trophy winner as the nation's top fullback, Bredeson is a two-time captain whose value lies in his elite run-blocking, consistently earning high PFF grades for his ability to pave lanes in short-yardage situations. While his career stats of 12 receptions for 132 yards won't jump off the page, his reliability as a lead blocker and special teams ace has made him an indispensable "glue guy." Currently projected as a sixth-round pick or high-priority free agent, scouts view him as a high-floor specialist whose physical "old-school" style and leadership are tailor-made for teams looking to bolster their ground game and special teams units.

name: Kejon Owens
summary: Kejon Owens (often referred to as Kevon Owens in draft circles) is a 5’11", 210-pound running back from FIU who skyrocketed up draft boards following a historic 2025 senior campaign. Named the CUSA Offensive Player of the Year, Owens finished the season ranked 10th nationally in rushing yards, headlined by dominant performances like his 195-yard outing against WKU. Scouts laud his elite "processing" and contact balance, describing him as a natural runner who seamlessly identifies cutback lanes and maintains momentum through glancing blows. While his top-end speed is respectable, his "piston-like" leg drive in short-yardage situations has defined his "bruiser" reputation. Currently projected as a Day 3 sleeper or priority free agent, Owens’ high-level production and reliable hands make him a potential rotational back for NFL teams seeking backfield depth.

name: Haynes King
summary: Haynes King (6’3/215) is a high-ceiling developmental prospect who pairs elite vertical speed and a quick release with natural leadership and "clutch" playmaking. While his athleticism allows him to extend plays and stress defenses, he struggles with inconsistent accuracy on short-to-intermediate throws and a "gunslinger" mentality that leads to costly turnovers. His lean frame raises significant durability concerns, especially given his tendency to abandon mechanics under pressure. Ultimately, King offers the raw tools of a franchise starter but requires a patient organization to refine his fundamentals; without significant development, his floor is that of a specialized gadget player rather than a reliable NFL centerpiece.

name: Kendrick Law
summary: Kendrick Law (5’11”, 185 lbs) adds speed and versatility to the receiver room, producing 600+ yards and explosive-play efficiency while aligning both outside and in motion. He accelerates quickly, tracks the ball well, and stresses defenses vertically and on crossers. Law can struggle against physical press coverage and isn’t a high-volume contested-catch option, but his speed changes coverage rules. He projects as a Round 6 pick, contributing early as a situational deep threat, gadget player, and return option, with upside in spread or play-action offenses.

name: Jaylon Guilbeau
summary: Jaylon Guilbeau (5’10/180 est.) appeared in Texas’ nickel and boundary packages in 2025, showing quick feet, competitive ball skills, and solid run support in rotational reps. Press technique at the line, mirroring underneath, and instincts in zone/match coverage showed promise. Athletic profile and competitive traits fit as a developmental corner with nickel/slot upside. Recovery speed and burst lag against vertical threats; tackling angles in open space remain inconsistent; technique lapses occur during coverage transitions. Day 3/UDFA projection in the 2026 NFL Draft with upside as depth corner or special teams contributor if strength is added and coverage details are refined.

name: Marcus Allen
summary: Marcus Allen (5’11/185 est.) contributed in North Carolina’s secondary rotation in 2025, showing quick feet, competitive ball skills, and solid run support in nickel and boundary snaps. Press technique at the line, mirroring underneath, and instincts in zone/match coverage showed promise. Developmental corner with nickel/slot upside. Recovery speed and burst lag against vertical threats; tackling angles in open space remain inconsistent; technique lapses occur during transitions. Day 3/UDFA projection in the 2026 NFL Draft with upside as depth corner or special teams contributor if strength is added and coverage details are refined.

name: Dominic Zvada
summary: Dominic Zvada (6’0/190 est.) served as Michigan’s starting kicker in 2025, showing strong leg strength, accuracy on field goals and kickoffs, and consistency in pressure situations. Developmental specialist with starter traits if range and touch are refined. Weaknesses include occasional misses from distance or in wind; kickoff hang time and placement need polish; limited experience in high-pressure playoff environments. Day 3/UDFA projection in the 2026 NFL Draft with upside as depth kicker or special teams contributor if leg strength and consistency translate.

name: Malik Benson
summary: Malik Benson (6’1/195 est.) transferred to Florida State in 2025 and served as a rotational boundary receiver, showing reliable hands, intermediate separation, and yards after catch on slants/screens. Route nuance and body control appeared in limited production. Developmental slot/outside receiver with dependable traits. Size aids contested-catch situations; production remains modest; press coverage at the line creates inconsistency. Day 3/UDFA projection in the 2026 NFL Draft with upside as depth piece or special teams contributor if releases are refined and strength is added.

name: David Gusta
summary: David Gusta is a veteran defensive lineman for the Kentucky Wildcats, having completed his senior season in 2025 as a key interior disruptor. Measuring 6'3" and 317 lbs, the Washington State transfer brought elite physicality and durability to the SEC, starting all 12 games and recording 12 tackles, 3.0 tackles for loss, and a sack. While his box score stats are modest, his impact is reflected in a high 84.4 PFF grade and a notable ability to force turnovers via quarterback hurries. A durable prospect with 44 career appearances and 36 starts, Gusta has secured invitations to the 2026 East-West Shrine Bowl and the NFL Scouting Combine. He is currently projected as a reliable mid-round selection for the upcoming 2026 NFL Draft.

name: Kentrel Bullock
summary: Kentrel Bullock is a 5'10", 205-pound "one-cut-and-go" runner from South Alabama who enters the 2026 draft cycle as a record-breaking production leader. After transferring from Ole Miss, Bullock cemented his legacy in 2025 by setting the Jaguars' single-season rushing record with 1,085 yards and 14 touchdowns, earning First-Team All-Sun Belt honors. Scouts are enamored with his explosive upfield burst and elite contact balance, noting his ability to remain upright through glancing blows while maintaining "track-sprinter" movement in the open field. While he has significantly improved his ball security—not fumbling once during his 218-carry senior campaign—questions remain regarding his hip fluidity and pass-protection ceiling. Currently projected as a Day 3 value pick (Round 6-7), Bullock’s invitation to the East-West Shrine Bowl suggests his physical, touchdown-scoring profile has high-priority interest from NFL gap-scheme offenses.

name: Keyshaun Elliott
summary: Keyshaun Elliott is a highly productive, physical middle linebacker who projects as a "tackling machine" and reliable run defender at the next level. Measuring 6'2" and 235 pounds, the senior finished his 2025 campaign leading the Sun Devils in both total tackles (98) and sacks (7.0), showcasing a rare ability to impact the game as both a downhill thumper and a versatile blitzer. Scouts praise his elite vision, quick trigger, and "green dot" leadership intangibles. While he displays excellent play strength and a long tackle radius, concerns linger regarding his sideline-to-sideline range and man-coverage upside against elite NFL athletes. Currently projected as a Day 3 prospect or late-riser, his stock will depend on athletic testing to prove he can remain a three-down asset.

name: Ben Bell
summary: Ben Bell (6’3/255 est.) rotated on Virginia Tech’s edge in 2025, adding tackles, TFLs, and pressures with quick first step and violent hand usage. Versatile alignment options and high motor showed in pass-rush and run-defense snaps. Developmental edge with starter traits if strength and bend improve. Bend and corner speed remain average vs. quicker tackles; pass-rush plan is power-heavy with limited counters; leverage issues occur when pad level rises. Mid-to-late Day 3 projection (Round 5-7) in the 2026 NFL Draft with upside as rotational disruptor in schemes that value motor and hand usage.

name: Ricardo Hallman
summary: Ricardo Hallman (5’11/190 est.) started at corner for Wisconsin in 2025, recording tackles, pass breakups, and interceptions while showing quick feet, competitive press technique, and instincts in zone/match coverage. Physicality in run support and ball skills stood out in boundary snaps. Developmental corner with outside/nickel upside. Recovery speed and burst lag against vertical threats; tackling angles in open space remain inconsistent; technique lapses occur during transitions. Mid-to-late Day 3 projection (Round 5-7) in the 2026 NFL Draft with upside as depth corner or special teams contributor if strength is added and coverage details are refined.

name: Avery Smith
summary: Avery Smith (5’11/185 est.) contributed in Toledo’s secondary rotation in 2025, showing quick feet, competitive ball skills, and solid run support in nickel and boundary snaps. Press technique at the line, mirroring underneath, and instincts in zone/match coverage appeared in limited production. Developmental corner with nickel/slot upside. Recovery speed and burst lag against vertical threats; tackling angles in open space remain inconsistent; technique lapses occur during transitions. Day 3/UDFA projection in the 2026 NFL Draft with upside as depth corner or special teams contributor if strength is added and coverage details are refined.

name: Brett Norfleet
summary: Brett Norfleet (6’5/245 est.) contributed as a rotational tight end for Missouri in 2025, showing receptions in seam and red-zone roles with reliable hands and blocking effort on short-yardage plays. Route nuance and leaping at the catch point appeared in limited production. Developmental in-line/move tight end with receiving traits and every-down value. Blocking technique and anchor remain inconsistent against power edges; height advantage is limited in contested situations; vertical speed to stretch seams is modest. Day 3 projection (Round 4-6) in the 2026 NFL Draft with upside as rotational TE2/TE3 if blocking hand placement is refined and strength is added.

name: Matthew Hibner
summary: Matthew Hibner is a 6'5", 252-pound "prototypical" tight end from SMU who has transformed from a Michigan special-teamer into a legitimate NFL prospect. Scouts at the 2026 Senior Bowl were impressed by his "smooth" athleticism and soft hands, specifically noting his ability to use his large frame to shield defensive backs and win contested catches in traffic. While he caught 72% of his targets with only one drop in 2025, his week in Mobile also highlighted a need for more consistency as a "road-grading" run blocker to match his technical in-line alignment. Currently a Day 3 riser (Round 4-6), Hibner’s combination of a 6.8 YAC average and high-tier athletic traits has him firmly on the radar of teams seeking a high-upside, versatile "Y" tight end.

name: Dominic Bailey
summary: Dominic Bailey (6’4/255 est.) rotated on Tennessee’s edge in 2025, adding tackles, TFLs, and pressures with quick first step and violent hand usage. Versatile alignment options and high motor showed in pass-rush and run-defense snaps. Developmental edge with starter traits if strength and bend improve. Bend and corner speed remain average vs. quicker tackles; pass-rush plan is power-heavy with limited counters; leverage issues occur when pad level rises. Mid-to-late Day 3 projection (Round 5-7) in the 2026 NFL Draft with upside as rotational disruptor in schemes that value motor and hand usage.

name: Jameson Geers
summary: Jameson Geers is a 6'5", 255-pound tight end from the Minnesota Golden Gophers who enters the 2026 draft cycle as a technically sound, "every-down" prospect. After a breakout 2024 season (290 yards, 4 TDs), Geers remained a fixture in Minnesota’s run-heavy scheme in 2025, recording 26 receptions for 206 yards and 4 touchdowns. Scouts highly value his "Big Ten toughness"—he is a polished in-line blocker who excels in gap-scheme runs and possesses the frame to handle NFL-level edge rushers. While he isn't a "dynamic" vertical threat, he has a knack for finding soft spots in zone coverage and was a favorite red-zone target for the Gophers. Having exhausted his eligibility, Geers is currently projected as a High-Priority Undrafted Free Agent (UDFA) or 7th-Round Flyer, with his path to a roster spot likely depending on his ability to prove himself as a versatile "Y" tight end and special teams contributor.

name: Jordan Dwyer
summary: Jordan Dwyer is a 6'0", 190-pound "possession-plus" receiver who transitioned seamlessly from a 1,192-yard sophomore season at Idaho to becoming the primary target at TCU in 2025. Starting all 12 games for the Horned Frogs, Dwyer recorded 54 receptions for 730 yards and 7 touchdowns, proving his ability to thrive against Power Four competition. Scouts are particularly high on his "contested-catch grit" and polished route-running, often highlighting his willingness to work in traffic and his "hustle" on extended plays. While he currently deals with a nagging foot injury expected to linger until March, his high-volume production and reliable hands have established him as a productive "X" or "Z" receiver. Currently projected as a Day 3 pick or priority free agent, Dwyer enters the draft cycle as a technically sound, high-floor prospect who elevates an offense through consistency and football intelligence.

name: Griffin Wilde
summary: Griffin Wilde is a 6'2", 200-pound wide receiver for the Northwestern Wildcats who emerged as one of the Big Ten’s most reliable targets in 2025. After transferring from South Dakota State—where he posted a 1,147-yard sophomore season—Wilde seamlessly transitioned to Power Four football, leading Northwestern with 71 receptions for 880 yards and 8 touchdowns. Scouts praise his "advanced" frame, catch-point strength, and the tactical route-running that earned him Third-team All-Big Ten honors. He capped his junior year as the MVP of the 2025 GameAbove Sports Bowl, solidifying his reputation as a big-game performer. While he has been a "deep cut" on some 2026 big boards, his consistent production and "X-receiver" size make him a Day 3 riser to watch as he prepares for his final collegiate season or potential draft entry.

name: Jalil Farooq
summary: Jalil Farooq (5’11”, 195 lbs) delivers steady, dependable production at wide receiver, totaling 600+ yards and multiple touchdowns while working inside and outside. He shows reliable hands, strong route discipline, and toughness as a blocker and over-the-middle target. Farooq lacks elite speed or suddenness, but his consistency shows up on tape. He earned All-Conference recognition and steady draft interest. He projects as a Round 5–6 selection, contributing early as a WR4, slot/outside hybrid, and special teams contributor, with value in timing-based offenses.

name: Toby Payne
summary: Toby Payne is a 6'4", 237-pound tight end for the Marshall Thundering Herd who enters the 2026 draft cycle as one of the most reliable "security blankets" in the Sun Belt. A West Virginia native, Payne took a significant step forward in 2025, recording 36 receptions for 387 yards and 3 touchdowns. Scouts value his "large-frame" reliability and his ability to work the intermediate parts of the field, particularly on "third-and-medium" situations where his 10.8 yards-per-catch average was vital for the Herd. While he isn't considered a "burner" tight end, Payne excels as an in-line blocker and has shown steady improvement in his lateral movement. Currently a Senior, he is projected as a Day 3 (Round 6-7) prospect or High-Priority Undrafted Free Agent (UDFA) for the 2026 NFL Draft.

name: Amare Thomas
summary: Amare Thomas is a 6'0", 205-pound wide receiver for the Houston Cougars who exploded onto the national stage in 2025 after transferring from UAB. A "volume-heavy" playmaker with a professional approach, Thomas led Houston’s aerial attack with 67 receptions for 966 yards and a staggering 12 touchdowns during his junior campaign. Scouts are primarily focused on his "football IQ" and versatility; coach Willie Fritz has lauded his ability to play all three receiver positions without a drop-off in detail. While he may lack the "twitchy" suddenness of smaller slot receivers, he compensates with elite hands, a polished release package, and a gritty playing style that led to 400+ yards after the catch. Having officially announced his return to Houston for 2026, Thomas enters the season as a premier Big 12 Offensive Player of the Year candidate and a projected Day 2 (Round 2-3) target for the 2027 NFL Draft.

name: Isaac Smith
summary: Isaac Smith (6’0/220) solidified his status as the SEC’s premier "enforcer" following a 2025 campaign at Mississippi State where he led the conference in tackles for a second consecutive year. This hybrid safety-linebacker operates with a "seek-and-destroy" mentality, combining elite 4.52-speed with the functional mass to stonewall Power-4 running backs in the hole. While scouts initially questioned his deep-third range, his two-interception performance during Senior Bowl week proved he possesses the diagnostic instincts to thrive as a pro-level "centerfield" general. Currently projected as a fringe first-round talent, Smith offers a rare blend of leadership, durability, and "whistle-to-whistle" motor. He is the high-floor defensive anchor every modern NFL coordinator covets for 2026.

name: Gunner Stockton
summary: Gunner Stockton (6’1/215 est.) served as Georgia’s backup quarterback in 2025, completing passes with good arm strength, quick release, and mobility to extend plays in limited action. Dual-threat traits and accuracy on timing routes appeared in spot duty. Developmental quarterback with backup potential. Decision-making under pressure and pre-snap processing remain inconsistent; happy feet and hold-the-ball tendencies surface against blitzes; arm talent is solid but not elite. Day 3/UDFA projection in the 2026 NFL Draft with upside as developmental backup if mechanics and reads are refined.

name: Trebor Pena
summary: Trebor Peña (5’11”, 190 lbs) operates primarily from the slot, posting 70+ receptions and 700+ yards while consistently winning with short-area quickness, pacing, and route intelligence. He shows a strong feel for zone coverage, reliable hands in traffic, and toughness working the middle. Peña lacks true burner speed and isn’t built to live outside, but his efficiency and football IQ stand out. He earned All-Conference recognition for production and consistency. He projects as a Round 5–6 pick, offering early contribution as a slot receiver, motion piece, and return option, with WR3 upside in rhythm-based passing games.

name: Conner Weigman
summary: Conner Weigman (6’3/220) successfully revitalized his draft stock during a stellar 2025 breakout at Houston after transferring from Texas A&M. This refined pocket commander recorded 2,711 passing yards and 36 total touchdowns, showcasing the elite "post-pressure" poise and rapid-fire processing that made him a five-star recruit. While his medical history—including multiple season-ending lower-body injuries—remains a primary concern for NFL front offices, his improved 700-yard rushing floor proved his mobility is fully restored. Currently vaulting into early Day 2 consideration, Weigman offers the "quarterback-friendly" decision-making and pro-style diagnostic skills needed to stabilize a professional huddle. He is the quintessential high-floor leader for any NFL team seeking a mature, pro-ready signal-caller in 2026.

name: Kevin Jennings
summary: Kevin Jennings (6’3/215 est.) served as Arkansas’ backup quarterback in 2025, completing passes with good arm strength, quick release, and mobility to extend plays in limited action. Dual-threat traits and accuracy on timing routes appeared in spot duty. Developmental quarterback with backup potential. Decision-making under pressure and pre-snap processing remain inconsistent; happy feet and hold-the-ball tendencies surface against blitzes; arm talent is solid but not elite. Day 3/UDFA projection in the 2026 NFL Draft with upside as developmental backup if mechanics and reads are refined.

name: Devon Dampier
summary: Devon Dampier is a 5'11", 210-pound dual-threat quarterback at Utah who solidified his status as one of the Big 12's most explosive playmakers during the 2025 season. After transferring from New Mexico, Dampier earned Big 12 Offensive Newcomer of the Year honors, passing for 2,490 yards and 24 touchdowns while adding 835 yards and 10 scores on the ground. Scouts praise his elite "high-end explosiveness" and vision as a runner, complemented by a significant jump in passing efficiency (63.5% completion rate). Despite early speculation regarding the NFL Draft or a follow-up transfer, Dampier officially announced in January 2026 that he is returning to Utah for his senior season. This decision positions him as a premier Heisman contender and a high-priority "riser" for the 2027 NFL Draft class.

name: Antonio Kite
summary: Antonio Kite (6’0/180) operates as a "fluid" boundary corner who salvaged his draft stock during a productive 2025 campaign at Ole Miss. This former Alabama transfer recorded five pass breakups, showcasing the hip flexibility and " basketball-style" ball skills required to neutralize vertical SEC threats. While scouts note his slender build creates an uphill battle against physical NFL "power" receivers, his 4.48-speed and natural diagnostic instincts solidify a mid-Day 3 grade. Kite offers the high-end movement skills and zone-scheme IQ needed to stabilize a pro secondary. He is a high-upside developmental prospect for 2026.

name: Dasan McCullough
summary: Dasan McCullough (6’4/240 est.) rotated at linebacker for Oklahoma in 2025, contributing solid range, tackling efficiency, and instincts in run defense and coverage. Sideline-to-sideline traits and pursuit motor appeared in limited snaps. Developmental off-ball linebacker with three-down potential. Physicality and shedding remain inconsistent against climbing blockers; slow reads and false steps occur in aggressive run fits; added strength and mass are needed to anchor in the box. Day 3 projection (Round 5-7) in the 2026 NFL Draft with upside as depth linebacker and special teams contributor if run-game technique improves.

name: Hykeem Williams
summary: Hykeem Williams (6’2/220) remains a "massive" high-ceiling prospect who showcased flashes of dominance during his 2025 season at Colorado. This former five-star recruit utilizes a prototypical NFL frame and "basketball-style" body control to dominate 50/50 scenarios and bully smaller corners at the catch point. While a mid-season injury limited his statistical output, his "catch-to-attack" mentality and elite strength-to-weight ratio make him a primary red-zone mismatch. Scouts in Mobile lauded his "violent" releases and refined route-stems, suggesting his best football is still ahead of him. Currently projected as a high Day 2 riser, Williams offers the physical "alpha" traits and massive catch radius required to become a primary boundary weapon in a pro-style offense.

name: Rueben Owens
summary: Rueben Owens II is a 5'11", 215-pound running back for the Texas A&M Aggies who enters the 2026 season as one of the most explosive "bounce-back" stories in the SEC. After missing nearly all of 2024 with a foot injury, Owens reclaimed his elite form in 2025, stepping into a primary role to rush for 639 yards and 5 touchdowns while adding 130 receiving yards. Scouts are enamored with his "game-breaking speed" (clocked at 21.5 MPH in-game) and his background as a world-class long jumper, which translates to elite lateral suddenness and contact balance. While he was eligible for the 2026 NFL Draft, Owens officially announced in January 2026 that he is returning to Texas A&M for his senior season. This move cements him as a projected Day 2 (Round 2-3) prospect for the 2027 NFL Draft and a centerpiece for the Aggies' championship aspirations.

name: Raylen Wilson
summary: Raylen Wilson (6’0”, 230 lbs) flies around the field as an aggressive linebacker, stacking up 100+ tackles, tackles for loss, and splash plays through instinct and closing speed. He triggers quickly downhill and shows comfort scraping laterally to cut off outside runs. Coverage remains functional but not a strength, especially against backs and tight ends in space. Wilson earned All-Conference recognition and steady draft buzz. He projects as a Round 4–5 pick, with immediate impact on special teams and early-down defense, and a path to starter reps in the right front.

name: Jordan Hall
summary: Jordan Hall (6’4”, 310 lbs) functions as a dependable interior defender, finishing with 35+ tackles and tackles for loss while anchoring against the run. He plays with good pad level, strong hands, and discipline, often absorbing double teams to free linebackers. Hall offers limited pass-rush juice, but his consistency stands out. He earned All-Conference honorable mention for steady play. He projects as a Round 6 pick, contributing early as a rotational run defender, particularly in early-down and short-yardage defensive packages.

name: Ayden Williams
summary: Ayden Williams is a 6'3", 205-pound junior wide receiver for the Mississippi State Bulldogs, having transferred from Ole Miss in January 2025. Originally a consensus four-star recruit and a 2022 Under Armour All-American from Ridgeland, Mississippi, Williams spent his first two collegiate seasons in Oxford, where he recorded a combined seven receptions for 119 yards across 19 games. Seeking more consistent playing time, he reunited with Bulldogs head coach Jeff Lebby—who had previously recruited him to Ole Miss—and saw significantly increased involvement during the 2025 season. Appearing in all 13 games for Mississippi State, Williams recorded 21 receptions for 252 yards, averaging 12 yards per catch, and notched a season-long 49-yard reception against Tennessee. Despite not finding the end zone in 2025, his size and vertical athleticism make him a key boundary weapon as he moves into his senior year in 2026.

name: Jaquaize Pettaway
summary: Jaquaize Pettaway is an explosive senior wide receiver for the East Carolina Pirates, having transferred from Oklahoma in early 2025. A former consensus four-star recruit from Houston, Texas, Pettaway arrived at ECU with a reputation for elite, track-star speed, underscored by a personal best of 10.41 seconds in the 100-meter dash. During his tenure at Oklahoma, he notably set a school record for receptions by a true freshman in a debut game, though he later sought a more consistent role in Greenville. Entering the 2026 season, the 5'10", 194-pound athlete remains a primary deep threat and slot option for the Pirates. His rare combination of vertical acceleration and open-field agility makes him a "cheat code" for the ECU offense.

name: Quinten Joyner
summary: Quinten Joyner (5’11/215) stands out as a "physical" downhill runner who spearheaded Texas Tech’s ground game with elite one-cut burst and track-star acceleration (10.99 100m). This "stout" playmaker excels when hitting alleys, utilizing "clear accelerating juice" and a 49-foot shot put strength to power through arm-tackles at the second level. While he occasionally runs too upright, leading to contact-balance inconsistencies, his high-volume production and rare athletic data make him a tantalizing Day 3 prospect. Following a productive 2025 campaign showcasing improved pass-catching exposure, Joyner projects as a high-upside rotational back. He possesses the "galloping" long speed and functional mass required to handle a professional workload and revolutionize a creative NFL running-back committee.

name: Shelton Sampson Jr.
summary: Shelton Sampson Jr. is a 6'4", 195-pound wide receiver for the Louisiana Ragin' Cajuns who re-established himself as a premier vertical threat in 2025. After transferring from LSU, Sampson stepped into a leading role in Lafayette, pacing the team with 32 receptions for 537 yards and 6 touchdowns. Scouts prize his rare combination of "projectable size" and elite long speed, noted by his ability to stretch the field with a 16.8 yards-per-catch average. While he possesses the high-point ability and catch radius to dominate in 50-50 ball scenarios, evaluators look for him to continue adding mass to his lean frame to improve his consistency against physical press coverage. Having officially decided to return to Louisiana for the 2026 season, Sampson enters the year as a top-tier Sun Belt Player of the Year candidate and a projected Day 2 (Round 2-3) prospect for the 2027 NFL Draft.

name: Djay Braswell
summary: Dontavius "Djay" Braswell is a 5'11", 205-pound running back with "track-star" speed who spent the 2025 season with the Georgia State Panthers after transferring from South Carolina. A former 4-star recruit and Georgia state 100m champion (10.72s), Braswell is prized for his "home-run" hitting acceleration and compact, physical frame. While he served in a backup role for the Panthers in 2025—totaling 121 yards from scrimmage—scouts remain intrigued by his raw athletic profile and "blitz-pickup" reliability in pass protection. Following the conclusion of the 2025 season, Braswell officially entered the transfer portal on January 8, 2026, making him a high-upside "speed" addition for programs needing a change-of-pace back with SEC-level pedigree. Currently viewed as a developmental Day 3 prospect or priority free agent for future drafts, his stock depends on finding a system that can consistently leverage his open-field explosiveness.

name: Raymond Cottrell
summary: Raymond Cottrell Jr. is a 6'3", 210-pound wide receiver who has become a prominent "small-school riser" after transferring to West Florida (DII) for the 2025 season. Originally a highly-coveted 4-star recruit who had stints at Texas A&M and Kentucky, Cottrell found his stride with the Argonauts, utilizing his "Power Four" frame and elite catch-point strength to dominate lower-level secondaries. Scouts prize his "pro-ready" build and his ability to pick up massive chunks of yardage after the catch, often running through arm tackles like a tight end. While he spent much of 2024 and 2025 rebuilding his profile away from the Power Five spotlight, he has landed on several 2026 NFL Draft Watch Lists as a priority small-school prospect. Currently viewed as a High-Priority Undrafted Free Agent (UDFA) or late Day 3 flyer, Cottrell is a classic "traits-based" sleeper whose NFL future depends on a strong showing at a Pro Day or a postseason all-star game like the Tropical Bowl.

name: Anthony Evans III
summary: Anthony Evans III is a 5'11", 180-pound "lightning-bolt" playmaker for Mississippi State who cemented his status as one of the SEC's most dangerous deep threats in 2025. After transferring from Georgia, Evans thrived in Jeff Lebby's high-tempo offense, hauling in 67 receptions for 831 yards and 4 touchdowns. Scouts are obsessed with his "Olympic-level" speed (clocked at a 10.27-second 100m in high school) and his ability to generate explosive plays, leading his team with 18 catches of 20+ yards last season. While he primarily lives in the slot, his value doubles as an elite return specialist, evidenced by his 97-yard punt return performance against Alcorn State. Despite heavy NFL interest as a potential Day 2 vertical threat, Evans officially announced in January 2026 that he is returning to Mississippi State for his senior year, making him the centerpiece of the Bulldogs' 2026 aerial attack and a top-tier prospect for the 2027 draft.

name: Shamar Easter
summary: Shamar Easter (6’5/225 est.) contributed as a rotational tight end for North Carolina in 2025, showing receptions in seam roles, good hands, and blocking effort on short-yardage plays. Route nuance and leaping at the catch point appeared in limited production. Developmental in-line/move tight end with receiving traits and every-down value. Blocking technique and anchor remain inconsistent against power edges; height advantage is limited in contested situations; vertical speed to stretch seams is modest. Day 3 projection (Round 5-7) in the 2026 NFL Draft with upside as rotational TE2/TE3 if blocking hand placement is refined and strength is added.

name: Braylon James
summary: Braylon James is a physically imposing 6'2", 190-pound wide receiver for the TCU Horned Frogs who is recognized for his elite vertical explosiveness and extensive catch radius. Originally a consensus four-star recruit from Round Rock, Texas, James began his collegiate career at Notre Dame in 2023, where he recorded a 12-yard reception against Pittsburgh, before transferring to TCU. Leveraging a standout track-and-field background that includes a 4.47-second 40-yard dash and a 38.4-inch vertical jump, he appeared in nine games for the Horned Frogs during the 2024 season. Although he remained on the roster as a sophomore in 2025, his on-field production was limited by injury, and he did not record official statistics that year. Despite these early challenges, James enters 2026 as a high-potential developmental prospect and a significant "big-play" threat capable of overpowering smaller defensive backs.

name: Nathan Leacock
summary: Nathan Leacock is a physically imposing 6'3", 217-pound redshirt sophomore wide receiver for the North Carolina Tar Heels who combines prototypical NFL size with verified elite track speed (sub-11.00s 100m). A former top-100 recruit, Leacock transferred to UNC in 2025 via Tennessee and Purdue, looking to leverage a massive 33-inch catch radius and high-pointing ability that makes him a premier red-zone threat. While his 2025 collegiate production remained modest as he adjusted to new systems, his film highlights a "bully-ball" style of play, using superior play strength to defeat press coverage and shield defenders on contested catches. To reach his ceiling as a Day 3 NFL prospect, Leacock must continue to refine his route-running efficiency and suddenness out of breaks, though his raw tools and "contested-catch alpha" potential remain among the highest in the ACC.

name: Samuel Singleton Jr.
summary: Samuel Singleton Jr. is a 5'11", 201-pound running back for the Florida State Seminoles who enters the 2026 season as the team's primary "home-run threat." After playing a supporting role in 2024, Singleton emerged in late 2025 as a hyper-efficient weapon, racking up 360 rushing yards and 4 touchdowns on just 51 carries (an elite 7.1 YPC). A former track star with a 10.86-second 100m dash, his game is built on world-class acceleration and the ability to disappear once he reaches the second level of the defense. Scouts view him as a "lightning" complement to a power-rushing attack, noting his improved vision and maturity during his 2025 breakout. Following the departure of veterans like Roydell Williams, Singleton is projected to take a massive leap in workload for 2026. He is currently considered a high-upside Day 3 prospect for the 2027 NFL Draft, with the potential to rise into Day 2 if he maintains his efficiency over a full season's volume.

name: Damari Brown
summary: Damari Brown (6’2/195) enters the 2026 cycle as a "high-IQ" cornerstone for Miami, having anchored the Hurricanes' secondary during their deep 2025 playoff run. This former American Heritage standout excels in patterns where he can use his elite length to "box out" receivers at the catch point.
