While evaluators note he must continue refining his lateral twitch to handle shifty slot weapons, his high-floor diagnostic skills and 22 career pass deflections highlight a pro-style starter's floor. Following a refined 2025 campaign where he was lauded for his leadership in the huddle, Brown has solidified a top-100 grade. He offers the rare combination of height, diagnostic instincts, and recovery burst required to anchor a modern professional sub-package defense.

name: Jaylon Braxton
summary: Jaylon Braxton (6’0/190) solidified his status as an "instinctive" defensive anchor for Ole Miss, recording an interception and 15 return yards during a standout 2025 campaign. This former four-star recruit utilizes elite 4.45-speed and "wide receiver-style" ball skills to generate turnovers, proving he is a true three-down "enforcer" in the SEC. While he occasionally struggles with over-aggressive pursuit angles, his pattern-matching agility and "click-and-close" diagnostic skills make him a defensive coordinator’s dream for multiple secondary roles. Currently projected as a consensus top-75 pick, Braxton offers the high-end processing and "alpha" mentality required to lead a professional huddle. He is a high-floor safety prospect with the versatility to play in the box or deep-third.

name: Cordale Russell
summary: Cordale Russell is a physically imposing 6'4", 210-pound wide receiver for Miami (OH) who entered the 2025 season as a high-upside transfer from Colorado and TCU. A former four-star prospect from North Mesquite High School, Russell is nicknamed "Baby TO" for his muscular frame, elite catch-in-traffic ability, and "my ball" mentality at the catch point. While undisclosed surgery sidelined him for the 2024 season, he remains a premier big-play threat characterized by a massive wingspan and 4.39-shuttle quickness. Though still refining his technical route-running and long speed, his combination of basketball-bred athleticism and dominant physicality makes him a mismatch on 50-50 balls with significant NFL developmental potential.

name: Ryan Niblett
summary: Ryan Niblett is a 5'10", 185-pound wide receiver and return specialist for the Texas Longhorns who transitioned from a "gadget" player to a consensus First-Team All-American in 2025. A former track phenom with sub-10.6 second 100m speed, Niblett has become one of the most feared "home-run" threats in the SEC. While he is primarily utilized as a slot receiver and "speed sweep" ball carrier, his greatest impact has been on special teams, where he led the nation in punt return yards last season. Despite heavy NFL Draft buzz following his breakout junior year, Niblett is officially returning to Texas for the 2026 season, where he is expected to serve as the primary explosive weapon for quarterback Arch Manning.

name: Dijon Johnson
summary: Dijon Johnson (6’1/204) functions as a prototypical boundary corner who utilized the 2025 season at Florida to showcase elite physical traits. This former top-100 recruit utilizes his massive reach and "violent" jam at the line of scrimmage to disrupt timing, recording an interception and multiple pass breakups in a condensed four-game window. While his season was limited by minor lower-body injuries, his "catch-to-attack" mentality and 4.48-speed profile make him a tantalizing Day 3 developmental project. Currently trending as a "high-upside" riser, Johnson offers the rare frame and functional play-strength needed to match up against the NFL’s larger perimeter threats. He is a high-ceiling prospect with the raw tools to eventually become a primary professional lockdown specialist.

name: Luke Hasz
summary: Luke Hasz (6’3/235 est.) contributed as a rotational tight end for Arkansas in 2025, showing receptions in seam and red-zone roles with reliable hands and blocking effort on short-yardage plays. Route nuance and leaping at the catch point appeared in limited production. Developmental in-line/move tight end with receiving traits and every-down value. Blocking technique and anchor remain inconsistent against power edges; height advantage is limited in contested situations; vertical speed to stretch seams is modest. Day 3 projection (Round 4-6) in the 2026 NFL Draft with upside as rotational TE2/TE3 if blocking hand placement is refined and strength is added.

name: Jalen Brown
summary: Jalen Brown is an elite vertical threat for Arkansas, characterized by his "first-class speed" and a background as a regional track champion. Standing 6-foot-1 and weighing 171 pounds, the redshirt sophomore transfer from Florida State and LSU excels at "taking the top off" defenses, specifically mastering fly and go routes. Scouts praise his smooth fluidity and ball-tracking ability, which allow him to make difficult over-the-shoulder catches. Before a serious leg injury (broken tibia and fibula) ended his 2025 season against Notre Dame, he had recorded 12 catches for 167 yards and two touchdowns. To reach his NFL potential, Brown must improve his play strength for effective blocking and refine his production on short to intermediate routes.

name: Kedrick Reescano
summary: Kedrick Reescano is a powerful 6'0", 214-pound running back for the Arizona Wildcats who transitioned into a significant leadership role and goal-line threat during the 2025 season. After transferring from Ole Miss as a top-ten portal prospect, the Texas native utilized his elite contact balance and track-and-field explosiveness to lead Arizona with nine rushing touchdowns, ranking eighth in the Big 12. Known for a bruising running style complemented by surprising lateral agility and a signature jump cut, he recorded 396 rushing yards and a standout 76.1 PFF offensive grade. Reescano’s combination of triple-jump athleticism and "hard-nosed" physicality has established him as a premier offensive weapon, highlighted by a dominant three-touchdown performance against Baylor that earned him national recognition.

name: Brandon Inniss
summary: Brandon Inniss is a dynamic 6'0", 199-pound wide receiver for the Ohio State Buckeyes who solidified his reputation as a technically elite playmaker during the 2025 season. Originally a five-star recruit from Florida, Inniss excels in the slot, utilizing advanced route-running and explosive releases to consistently create separation. Known for his "alpha" competitive temperament, he displays remarkable toughness and reliable hands, particularly in high-traffic areas where his yards-after-catch ability shines. Beyond his 36 receptions and three touchdowns in 2025, Inniss served as a dual-threat special teams weapon, recording over 240 return yards. His combination of tactical intelligence, sudden change-of-direction, and physical play style makes him a vital offensive pillar and a high-floor prospect with significant professional potential.

name: Jacobe Johnson
summary: Jacobe Johnson (6’2/200) solidified his status as a "high-IQ" defensive cornerstone for Oklahoma, recording two interceptions and 18 tackles during the 2025 season. This former four-star recruit utilizes elite 4.45-speed and "wide receiver-style" hands to generate turnovers, earning a reputation as one of the Big 12's most instinctive secondary generals. While scouts note he must continue refining his pursuit angles in run support, his ability to play both corner and safety with "telepathic" diagnostic skills makes him a defensive coordinator’s dream. Currently projected as a Day 2 value, Johnson offers the rare combination of length, diagnostic instincts, and recovery burst needed to lead an NFL secondary. He is a pro-ready defensive general with the IQ to stabilize a defense.

name: London Humphreys
summary: London Humphreys (6’2/200) serves as a "lightning-fast" vertical weapon for Georgia, having recorded 276 yards and three touchdowns on just 18 receptions in 2025. This Vanderbilt transfer excels at stretching the intermediate seam, utilizing his track-star background to record 15.33 yards per reception. While he has primarily functioned as a rotational "Z" receiver, his "dominant" performances against ranked opponents—including a crucial score against Texas—highlight a high-end NFL starter's floor. Currently vaulting into Day 2 consideration, Humphreys offers the rare vertical twitch and route-running savvy required to become a primary deep-threat for a professional offense. He is a premiere "home-run" specialist with the speed to turn routine vertical stems into game-changing scores.

name: Sedrick Alexander
summary: Sedrick Alexander is a powerhouse senior running back for the Vanderbilt Commodores, recognized as a cornerstone of the program's modern "Golden Era." A native of Austin, Texas, the 5'9", 207-pound back combines elite SEC physicality with refined pass-protection skills and a versatile receiving game. Alexander capped off a stellar 2025 campaign with 570 rushing yards and 11 touchdowns, highlighted by a dominant 115-yard performance in a historic victory over Tennessee. Entering the 2026 season as a Doak Walker Award candidate, he passed up the NFL Draft to provide veteran leadership for a high-voltage offense. Beyond his on-field production, Alexander is celebrated for his infectious humor and steadfast loyalty, having remained with the Commodores throughout their rise to national prominence.

name: Hank Brown
summary: Hank Brown is a 6'4", 215-pound quarterback currently with the Iowa Hawkeyes who remains one of the most intriguing "arm-talent" projects in the Big Ten. After starting the 2024 season at Auburn—where he flashed elite potential with a four-touchdown performance against New Mexico before struggling in SEC play—Brown transferred to Iowa for the 2025 season. During his first year in Iowa City, he served primarily in a developmental backup role, completing 11-of-21 passes for 107 yards and a touchdown across three appearances. Scouts view Brown as a classic "pure pocket passer" with an NFL-caliber frame and a high-velocity release, though he is still working to improve his decision-making under pressure and overall processing speed. Heading into the 2026 season, Brown is competing for the Hawkeyes' starting job, with his draft stock currently projected as a Day 3 developmental prospect for the 2027 cycle.

name: Grayson Loftis
summary: Grayson Loftis is a 6'3", 215-pound quarterback for the Charlotte 49ers who enters the 2026 spring season in a pivotal competition for the starting job. A former Duke transfer with ACC starting experience, Loftis was thrust into the QB1 role in 2025 following a season-ending injury to starter Conner Harrell. While he showcased a "pro-caliber" frame and tireless work ethic, he struggled with consistency in a difficult 0–11 campaign as a starter, throwing for 1,415 yards, 8 touchdowns, and 8 interceptions.

name: Kaden Feagin
summary: Kaden Feagin is a rare physical specimen who projects as a versatile, "dominant" weapon following his 2026 transition from running back to tight end. At 6'3" and 255 pounds, he possesses the frame of a modern NFL H-back combined with elite athleticism, evidenced by a documented top speed of over 21 mph. His greatest strengths are his power through contact and his proficiency as a pass-catcher in space, where he is a mismatch for most linebackers. While he is a menacing downhill runner with excellent balance, his primary developmental focus is refining his blocking technique and fundamental footwork for his new position. If he can leverage his natural strength to become a consistent blocker, Feagin’s unique blend of size and explosive burst makes him a high-upside hybrid prospect.

name: TreVonte Citizen
summary: TreVonte Citizen is a powerful 6'2", 223 lbs running back who returned to the spotlight at McNeese State in 2025 after an injury-plagued start to his career at Miami. A former consensus four-star recruit, Citizen is a prototypical "linear slasher" who utilizes his dense, proportional frame to absorb contact and finish runs with significant forward lean. Despite his size, he possesses verified top-end speed, bolstered by a background in track, and excels in one-cut situations where he can accelerate through gaps. While he proved his durability in 2025 with 503 rushing yards and four touchdowns, scouts look for continued growth in pass protection and lateral agility. His combination of low mileage and "Power Five" physical tools makes him an intriguing, high-floor developmental prospect for the 2026 NFL Draft.

name: Jaylon Glover
summary: Jaylon Glover stands as a pivotal figure for the UNLV Rebels football program following a standout 2025 campaign. The senior running back, a former Florida "Mr. Football" winner and Utah transfer, proved his versatility last season by accumulating 747 all-purpose yards. Glover’s efficiency was evident in his 6.0 yards per carry, anchored by a season-high 112-yard performance against Boise State. Beyond his rushing prowess, he emerged as a reliable special teams asset, contributing 312 kick return yards. With his combination of elite speed and veteran leadership, Glover is projected to be the cornerstone of the Rebels' offense in 2026, as they aim for a Mountain West title and a high-tier bowl appearance.

name: Maalik Murphy
summary: Maalik Murphy is a prototypical pocket passer standing 6'5" and weighing 234 lbs, possessing one of the most powerful arms in the 2026 NFL Draft class. Currently a senior at Oregon State after stints at Texas and Duke, Murphy is a "high-ceiling" prospect known for an effortless release and the ability to drive the ball into tight windows. While his 93.5% arm talent allows for elite deep-ball velocity and back-shoulder placement, he remains a developmental "thrower over runner" with limited mobility. His scouting profile highlights a need for improved decision-making and consistency on short-yardage touch, as his "big-game hunter" mentality occasionally leads to risky interceptions. As a natural leader with a "cannon" arm, Murphy projects as a high-upside vertical threat at the next level.

name: Nate Johnson
summary: Nate Johnson is an explosive dual-threat quarterback and versatile athlete who recently returned to the Utah Utes after a stint at Vanderbilt. Standing 6-foot-1 and weighing approximately 200 pounds, Johnson’s greatest asset is his elite, track-level speed; he famously clocked a 10.56-second 100m dash in high school. This speed translates into a dynamic rushing ability, allowing him to serve as a home-run threat on read-option plays and designed runs. While his passing volume has been limited, he possesses a strong arm and a smooth, consistent release that hints at high developmental upside. As he navigates the 2026 draft cycle, scouts view him as a high-ceiling prospect who can stress defenses with his legs while continuing to refine his pocket presence and downfield accuracy.

name: Owen McCown
summary: Owen McCown is a savvy left-handed quarterback for the UTSA Roadrunners with the "coach on the field" mentality you'd expect from the son of 18-year NFL veteran Josh McCown. Standing 6-foot-2 and 190 pounds, McCown is a rhythm passer who excels in a spread system, evidenced by his program-record 93.9% completion rate (31-of-33) against Tulane in 2025. He lacks elite physical traits or a "cannon" arm, but he compensates with exceptional anticipation, pocket mobility, and decision-making, finishing his 2025 junior campaign with 3,019 total yards and a stellar 30-to-7 touchdown-to-interception ratio. While he needs to add bulk to survive NFL-level hits, his pedigree and ability to dissect defenses from the pocket make him one of the most intriguing "sleeper" prospects for the 2026 NFL Draft.

name: Noah Fifta
summary: Noah Fifita (5’10/195) functions as a "masterclass" field general who utilized his 2025 season at Arizona to record 3,228 yards and 29 touchdowns. This First-Team All-Big 12 selection operates with elite "post-pressure" poise, utilizing a rapid-fire release and a 147.16 passer rating to dissect complex modern coverages. While his diminutive frame remains a primary "minefield" for NFL evaluators, his 64.3% completion rate and "quarterback-friendly" decision-making have made him a favorite for West Coast offensive coordinators. Following a refined 2025 campaign where he showcased improved "scramble drill" vision, Fifita has solidified a Day 3 grade. He possesses the elite processing speed and veteran "cool" required to lead a professional huddle in 2026.

name: Tory Grimes
summary: Tory Grimes (6’5/240) emerged as a "high-octane" edge presence for UNLV in 2025, utilizing a slippery speed-to-power transition to harass Group of Five quarterbacks. This long-limbed disruptor combines "violent" hand-usage with a "non-stop" motor, recording multiple tackles for loss and showcasing the bend needed to "run the hoop" against elite tackles. While scouts note he must continue adding functional mass to consistently stack-and-shed physical NFL "power" blockers, his elite pursuit range and diagnostic instincts highlight a high-upside developmental floor. Currently projected as a late-round value, Grimes offers the rare competitive "grit" and technical savvy required to impact an NFL rotation immediately. He is a high-upside developmental prospect with the motor to become a double-digit sack artist.

name: Alex McLaughlin
summary: Alex McLaughlin (6’2/190) stands out as a "high-octane" defensive playmaker who utilized a stellar 2025 season at Washington to showcase elite multi-alignment utility. Recording 96 total tackles and five sacks during his sophomore breakout, this former Northern Arizona star combines "telepathic" diagnostic instincts with the recovery speed needed to pattern-match elite Big Ten wideouts. While scouts note he must continue refining his pursuit angles to become a more consistent open-field tackler, his four interceptions and "whistle-to-whistle" motor highlight a high-end starter's floor. Currently projected as a high-floor third-round value, McLaughlin offers the rare frame and "ball-hawk" mentality required to lead an NFL secondary. He is a pro-ready "nickel" hybrid with the IQ to stabilize a modern defense.

name: Arion Carter
summary: Arion Carter (6’1/235) functions as a "violent" high-IQ linebacker who spearheaded Tennessee’s defense with 76 tackles and a Second-Team All-SEC nod in 2025. This former "Mr. Football" winner possesses elite sideline-to-sideline range, evidenced by a 17-tackle masterpiece against Georgia. While a significant turf toe injury forced him to withdraw from the 2026 NFL Draft and return to Knoxville, his 4.5-speed profile and "Patrick Queen-style" explosive burst remain his calling cards. Scouts laud his ability to communicate complex front adjustments and navigate heavy traffic with refined diagnostic instincts. If he recovers fully from surgery, Carter projects as a potential 2027 first-round cornerstone. He is the heart of the Vols' defensive identity heading into next season.

name: Channing Canada
summary: Channing Canada (6’3/245) lined up in linebacker rotation during 2025, displaying solid range, tackling efficiency, and instincts in run defense and coverage. Sideline-to-sideline pursuit and motor traits appeared in limited action. Developmental off-ball linebacker with three-down upside. Shedding climbing blockers remains inconsistent; aggressive run fits produce slow reads/false steps; added mass and strength required to anchor in the box. Day 3 projection (Round 5-7) in the 2026 NFL Draft—depth linebacker and special teams contributor if run-game technique and bulk improve.

name: Christian Gray
summary: Christian Gray (5’11/190 est.) started at corner for Notre Dame in 2025, recording tackles, pass breakups, and interceptions with quick feet, competitive press technique, and zone/match instincts. Physical run support and ball skills stood out in boundary snaps. Developmental corner with outside/nickel upside. Recovery speed and burst lag vs vertical threats; open-field tackling angles inconsistent; coverage transitions occasionally expose technique gaps. Mid-to-late Day 3 projection (Round 5-7) in the 2026 NFL Draft—depth corner or special teams contributor if strength improves and coverage polish increases.

name: Dean Miller
summary: Dean Miller is an explosive weak-side defensive end who transformed from a 200-pound JUCO transfer into an All-Big 12 Second Team standout. Standing 6'5" and weighing approximately 240 lbs, his greatest strength is his elite athleticism; he has been clocked at a top speed of 21 mph, rivaling skill position players. His high motor and improved play strength allowed him to record 6 sacks and 10 TFLs in his breakout 2024 season. However, Miller’s primary weakness remains his lack of ideal mass for the NFL, as he has historically struggled to maintain weight and can be outmuscled by heavier offensive tackles. Currently projected as a Day 3 pick or priority undrafted free agent, his draft stock hinges on proving he can anchor against the run at the professional level.

name: Deven Eastern
summary: Deven Eastern (6’6/320) provides a massive, high-ceiling presence on the interior, capping his Minnesota career with a career-high 499 snaps and 38 tackles in 2025. This "long-limbed" disruptor utilizes rare reach and a relentless motor to reset the line of scrimmage and collapse pocket integrity. While his pass-rush arsenal remains power-centric with limited lateral twitch, his ability to stack-and-shed double teams makes him a scheme-diverse asset. After a strong Senior Bowl where he showcased elite "knock-back" strength, Eastern’s stock has risen into the Day 3 conversation. He is a pro-ready anchor with immediate rotational value.

name: Devin Hobbs
summary: Daevin Hobbs (6’4/285) remains one of the most intriguing "freak" athletes in the 2026 class, entering his junior campaign at Tennessee as a primary defensive cornerstone. This former basketball standout utilizes an 80-inch wingspan and a verified 4.7 40-yard dash to displace interior linemen and "run the hoop" with rare fluidity. While he is still developing his technical "pass-rush" move set, his 20.5 TFLs as a high school junior and improved diagnostic instincts against SEC double-teams highlight a legitimate first-round ceiling. Currently trending as a high Day 2 riser, Hobbs offers the rare strength-speed combination required to excel at both the 5- and 7-technique. He is a high-upside disruptor with the raw physical tools to dominate pro-style offensive fronts.

name: Ethan Conner
summary: Ethan Conner (6’4/225) reinvented his professional outlook at Ole Miss, successfully transitioning from a dual-threat quarterback into a dynamic "Big-Slot" receiver for the 2025 season. This former JUCO standout utilizes his "above-the-rim" body control and deceptive 4.52-speed to dominate smaller defensive backs in contested scenarios. While he is still refining his nuance as a route-runner, his ability to "box out" defenders in the red zone and operate as a vertical seam-stretcher makes him a defensive coordinator’s nightmare. Currently projected as a late-round value, Conner offers the rare combination of athletic dominance and positional versatility required to thrive in a modern creative offense. He is a premiere offensive "chess piece" for the 2026 cycle.

name: Jaden Greathouse
summary: Jaden Greathouse (6’2”, 210 lbs) brings a pro-ready frame and physical receiving style, posting 700+ yards and multiple touchdowns while frequently drawing top coverage. He excels at shielding defenders, winning contested catches, and working the middle of the field. Greathouse plays with confidence and toughness but can struggle to separate consistently against quick, technical corners. He did not appear in a Senior Bowl setting, but his production earned All-Conference recognition. Teams will value his reliability and size, projecting him as a boundary receiver who can contribute early in red-zone and possession roles.

name: Jamari Johnson
summary: Jamari Johnson (5’11/185 est.) appeared in a secondary rotation during 2025, displaying quick feet, competitive ball skills, and solid run support in nickel and boundary snaps. Press technique at the line, mirroring underneath, and instincts in zone/match coverage showed promise. Developmental corner with nickel/slot upside. Recovery speed and burst lag against vertical threats; tackling angles in open space remain inconsistent; technique lapses occur during transitions. Day 3/UDFA projection in the 2026 NFL Draft with upside as depth corner or special teams contributor if strength is added and coverage details are refined.

name: Lawson Luckie
summary: Lawson Luckie (6’4/240) enters the 2026 draft cycle as Georgia’s next "super-prospect" at tight end after returning for his senior season. Following a breakout 2025 campaign where he led all Bulldog tight ends with 348 yards and a record-setting three-touchdown performance against Ole Miss, Luckie has cemented his status as a premiere vertical mismatch. He combines elite 14.5 yards-per-catch utility with "alpha" toughness and a high-IQ approach to pattern-matching. While his in-line blocking remains a secondary trait, his "above-the-rim" body control and straight-line burst make him a primary first-round candidate. Luckie offers the rare combination of athletic dominance and veteran leadership required to become a franchise safety-valve. He is a premiere modern tight-end weapon.

name: Ahmad Moten Sr.
summary: Ahmad Moten Sr. (6’3/290 est.) rotated in Miami’s interior snaps in 2025, adding tackles, TFLs, and pocket pressure in run and pass phases. Quickness for size, active hands to disengage, and power to anchor against doubles stood out when pad level stayed low. Developmental 3-technique/nose tackle with multi-alignment value and high motor in short bursts. Leverage issues arise when pad level rises against climbing blockers; pass-rush counters are limited with little bend against quicker guards; stamina drops in extended reps. Day 3 projection (Round 5-7) in the 2026 NFL Draft with upside as rotational depth if technique is refined and strength is added.
